title: Large Sloped-Roof Dog House
summary: A 4ft x 4ft framed dog house with a sloped, shingled roof — sized for a large breed.
description:
Most small dog-house plans are really cat-house plans with a bigger door. This one is framed like an actual small building: a 2x4 floor frame, 2x2 stick-framed walls, and a real shingled roof over plywood decking — sized 4ft x 4ft for a large dog to turn around and lie down in.

The **sloped roof isn't decorative** — the 7.5° angle cuts on the side wall studs are what shed rain off the high side instead of pooling on a flat roof, which is exactly how a flat-roofed dog house rots out from the top down in two winters.

Build the walls and roof frame first and dry-fit everything before you commit to cutting the siding — stick-framed angles compound fast, and it's much cheaper to catch an error in construction lumber than in a sheet of siding.

tools.note: Handles both the square crosscuts and the 7.5° angled cuts on the side walls.

materials.name: T1-11 exterior siding, 4x8 sheet
materials.note: Cut into the front, back, and two side wall panels.

materials.name: Asphalt shingles, roofing felt, and drip edge
materials.note: A real shingled roof, not a shortcut — it's what actually keeps the inside dry.

cutList.note: 7.5° angle both ends — ties the two side wall slopes together at the peak.

steps.title: Build the floor frame
steps.body:
Assemble the floor frame from the 2x4 sides and ends, then cut and screw down the 3/4" plywood floor deck.

Get the frame square here — it's the reference every wall frame above it registers off of.

steps.title: Build the front and back wall frames
steps.body: Build the front and back wall stud frames flat on the ground from the 2x2 studs per the cut list, then stand them on the floor deck and screw them down.

steps.title: Build and install the angled side walls
steps.body:
Cut the side wall studs, angling the ends 7.5° exactly where the cut list calls for it — these angles are what create the roof slope, so cut one test piece and check the angle before running the rest.

Assemble each side frame flat, then stand both between the front and back walls and screw them in.

steps.title: Install the roof ridge
steps.body: Cut the 2x2 ridge piece with a 7.5° angle on both ends and install it at the peak, tying the two side wall slopes together.

steps.title: Side and trim the exterior
steps.body: Cut the T1-11 siding panels to fit the front, back, and side wall frames and nail them on. Add the 1x4 trim boards around the corners and openings to hide the raw plywood edges.

steps.title: Deck and shingle the roof
steps.body:
Cut and install the 1/2" plywood roof deck over the ridge and side walls. Roll out roofing felt, install drip edge along the eaves, then shingle from the bottom edge up, overlapping each course.

Don't skip the felt underlayment to save time — it's the difference between a roof that sheds water and one that just slows it down.
materials: Asphalt shingles, roofing felt, and drip edge
